# Test data

This directory holds static fixtures consumed by the test suite. Each
subdirectory is described below. The fixtures grow over the build phases;
Stage 1 ships the directory layout and conventions, with content filled in
during Stage 2 and beyond.

## `registries/`

Sample artifact registries used as inputs to the test suite.

- `reference/`  the kitchen-sink registry covering every first-class type,
  every visibility mode, every adapter, every error path. Loaded by adapter
  conformance, end-to-end scenarios, and integration tests that need
  realistic data.
- `visibility/`  one minimal registry per visibility scenario. Each
  scenario asserts a specific row of the §11 visibility test list.
- `extends/`  parent and child manifests for layered `extends:` scenarios.
- `lint/`  paired good and bad registries per lint rule. The bad registries
  carry a single intentional violation; the test asserts the lint rule
  fires with the expected diagnostic.

## `manifests/`

Individual `ARTIFACT.md` and `SKILL.md` fixtures used by parser tests that
do not need a full registry context.

## `webhooks/`

Signed webhook payloads from each `GitProvider`. Stored in
`<provider>/<event>.json` plus a matching `<provider>/<event>.sig` produced
under a known HMAC secret. Negative payloads carry the suffix
`.invalid-sig.json`.

## `oidc/`

Signing key pairs, JWKS documents, and sample OIDC tokens for representative
identities. Tokens are regenerated by `tools/oidc/regen` (lands in Stage 4)
under a frozen clock, so they stay deterministic across test runs.

## `golden/`

Expected outputs checked via `internal/testharness.AssertGoldenFile`.

- `adapter/<adapter>/<fixture>/`  expected file tree per adapter and fixture.
- `lock/`  expected `.podium/sync.lock` files per scenario.
- `audit/`  expected audit-event records.
- `responses/`  expected HTTP response payloads.
- `errors/`  expected error envelopes for each namespaced error code.

## Conventions

- Fixtures are deterministic. Generators (token refresh, signature
  computation) read a frozen clock and a seeded RNG.
- Updates to golden files go through `make update-golden`. Reviewers see the
  diff in PRs.
- Each fixture file carries a one-line comment header naming the spec
  section it exercises.
